query: html anchor target attribute interview question

competitors.label: HTMLAnchorElement: target property
competitors.selectionReason: This is the canonical MDN reference for the anchor target property and the supported browsing-context values such as _self, _blank, _parent, and _top.
theirStrengths: MDN is authoritative on the actual target values and on how the browser interprets browsing contexts for linked resources.
theirGaps: The API-style reference is less interview-shaped and less explicit about UX trade-offs, new-tab overuse, or the security follow-up teams usually miss.

realWorldUseCases: New tabs are a UX and security trade-off, so test keyboard flow and rel attributes.
followUpConfusion: For _blank, add rel="noopener noreferrer" for security.

realWorldUseCases: MDN defines the target values precisely, but it is lighter on when a new tab is actually justified and how that affects user flow.
actionableExamples: It includes direct target examples and value lists, so the example criterion stays competitive.
followUpConfusion: Our page is more explicit about the real follow-up confusion: _blank security, overusing new tabs, and user-expectation trade-offs.

actionableExamples: Keep the practical scenario and the rel reminder together so the page stays operational, not only definitional.

competitors.winReason: MDN is the canonical target reference, but our page is stronger as an interview answer because it turns target into a UX and security trade-off rather than stopping at the reserved keywords.
